conflict: Doug's realization that his drunken toast exists as a public artifact he cannot retract
consequence: Doug goes from a man whose interior life is private to a man whose interior has been broadcast without his sober consent — his containment is permanently breached
must_land_beats: Doug wakes hungover in suit pants and one shoe, phone in bathroom sink with 47 missed calls
Doug watches the full toast video for the first time — the reader experiences it with him
The toast oscillates between savage, pathetic, hilarious, and devastating — the 'table' line lands as the emotional anchor
Doug watches the video a second time and his reaction shifts from horror to something more complicated
The 310 area code call that Doug doesn't answer — the outside world arriving
pressure_source: Doug's realization that his drunken toast exists as a public artifact he cannot retract
state_shift: Doug goes from a man whose interior life is private to a man whose interior has been broadcast without his sober consent — his containment is permanently breached
